title: git_repository
description: One source-code repository on any git hosting platform — GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, self-hosted Gitea, Azure DevOps, AWS CodeCommit. The single cross-vendor shape policies use for branch-protection, review, and lifecycle checks. Plugins normalize platform-specific protection-rule configurations into the boolean and integer fields here.

name.description: Repository name (typically `org/repo` or just `repo` — plugin's choice; must be stable per repo).

default_branch.description: Name of the repo's default branch (main, master, trunk, …).

default_branch_protected.description: Default branch has at least one protection rule enabled (any kind — review requirement, push restriction, status check).

required_reviewers_count.description: Minimum approving reviewers required on default-branch merges. 0 when not enforced.

requires_linear_history.description: Default-branch protection forbids merge commits (squash/rebase only).

allows_force_push.description: Force-push is permitted on the default branch (red flag for audit; usually false in a protected repo).

archived.description: Repository is archived (read-only, no new activity).

secret_scanning_enabled.description: Secret scanning is enabled on the repository.

push_protection_enabled.description: Push protection (blocking secrets on push) is enabled.

dismiss_stale_reviews.description: Approved reviews are dismissed when new commits are pushed.

require_code_owner_reviews.description: Code owner review is required for changes to owned paths.
